content: GTK theme live-switching on Wayland requires xdg-desktop-portal-gtk running so gsettings changes propagate; gsettings is a frontend over dconf. Matugen supports post-hook scripts that run gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface gtk-theme/color-scheme and icon-theme after generating colors, which is the standard way to make matugen-driven themes also flip GTK3/4 apps. Apps must be running with the portal and honoring gtk-theme name; XFCE apps (Thunar) use xfconf/xsettingsd path instead of pure gsettings, which is a likely root cause for Thunar not re-theming.
